name: invariant-sdk
documentation:
This contains the Python SDK for interacting with the [Invariantlabs](https://invariantlabs.ai/) APIs.

For more details on the operations and to get started, visit the [docs](https://explorer.invariantlabs.ai/docs/).

## Install via pip
```bash
pip install invariant-sdk
```

## Run tests
1. To run tests run `pytest`.
2. To run tests with coverage run `pytest --cov=invariant_sdk`.
3. To run tests with coverage (to also include a HTML report) run `pytest --cov=invariant_sdk --cov-report=html`. You can then open the `index.html` file in the `htmlcov` folder in your browser to see more coverage details by functions or classes.

For additional details add the `-s` and `-vv` flags when running pytest:
- `-s`: Allowing you to see print statements and other standard output.
- `-vv`: Increase verbosity, providing more detailed information about each test.

modules.name: invariant_sdk.utils
documentation: Utility functions for the Invariant SDK.

functions.name: invariant_sdk.utils.fetch_env_var
documentation:
Retrieve an environment variable's value.

This function attempts to get the value of the environment variable specified by `name`.
If the environment variable is not set, it returns the provided `default` value.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
    default (Optional[str]): The value to return if the environment variable is not set.
                             Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The value of the environment variable, or the `default` value if the
                   environment variable is not set.

functions.name: invariant_sdk.utils.get_api_url
documentation:
Retrieve the API url from the provided value or from the environment variable.

Args:
    api_url (Optional[str]): The API url provided as an argument. If None, the
                             function will attempt to fetch it from the environment.

Returns:
    str: The API url, stripped of any leading or trailing whitespace.

Raises:
    InvariantUserError: If the API url is empty or contains only whitespace.

functions.name: invariant_sdk.utils.get_api_key
documentation:
Retrieve the API key from the provided value or from the environment variable.

Args:
    api_key (Optional[str]): The API key provided as an argument. If None, the
                             function will attempt to fetch it from the environment.

Returns:
    str: The API key, stripped of any leading or trailing whitespace.

Raises:
    InvariantUserError: If the API key is empty or contains only whitespace.

documentation: Model class for the PushTraces API.

Convert the PushTracesRequest instance to a JSON-serializable dictionary.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A JSON-serializable dictionary representing the PushTracesRequest instance.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.types.push_traces.PushTracesRequest.validate_fields
documentation:
Validate the fields of the PushTracesRequest object.

Args:
    messages (List[List[Trace]]): The messages to validate.
    annotations (Optional[List[AnnotationCreate]]): The annotations to validate.
    metadata (Optional[List[Metadata]]): The metadata to validate.
    dataset (Optional[str]): The dataset name to validate.

Raises:
    ValueError: If any validation fails.
Model class which holds the PushTraces API request.

modules.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions
documentation: Describes the exceptions that can be raised by the Invariant SDK.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantError
documentation: An error occurred while communicating with the Invariant API.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantAPIError
documentation: Internal server error while communicating with Invariant.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantUserError
documentation: User error caused an exception when communicating with Invariant.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantAuthError
documentation: Couldn't authenticate with the Invariant API.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantNotFoundError
documentation: Couldn't find the requested resource.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.types.exceptions.InvariantAPITimeoutError
documentation: Request to the Invariant API timed out.

modules.name: invariant_sdk.client
documentation: Client for interacting with the Invariant APIs.

functions.name: invariant_sdk.client._close_session
documentation:
Close the given requests session.

This function ensures that the provided requests session is properly closed,
releasing any resources associated with it.

Args:
    session (requests.Session): The requests session to be closed.

classes.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a Client instance.

Args:
    api_url (Optional[str]): URL for the Invariant API. Defaults to the
                            INVARIANT_API_ENDPOINT environment variable or
                            DEFAULT_INVARIANT_API_URL if not set.
    api_key (Optional[str]): API key for the Invariant API. Defaults to the
                            INVARIANT_API_KEY environment variable.
    timeout_ms (Optional[Union[int, Tuple[int, int]]]): Timeout for API requests
                            in milliseconds. If it is a single integer, that
                            value is set as both the connect timeout and the
                            read timeout value. Otherwise it is a tuple specifying
                            (connect_timeout, read_timeout). Defaults to
                            DEFAULT_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS and DEFAULT_READ_TIMEOUT_MS.
    session Optional[Session]: The session to use for requests. If None, a new
                             session will be created.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client._headers
documentation:
Generates the headers required for making API requests.

This property constructs a dictionary containing the necessary headers
for authorization and content type for API requests.

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: A dictionary with the following headers:
        - "Authorization": A Bearer token for API authentication.
        - "Accept": Specifies that the response should be in JSON format.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.request
documentation:
Makes a request to the Invariant API.

Args:
    method (Literal["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]): The HTTP method to use
                                                      for the request.
    pathname (str): The path to make the request to.
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                              the requests method.

Returns:
    requests.Response: The response from the API.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.push_trace
documentation:
Push trace data to the Invariant API.

Args:
    request (PushTracesRequest): The request object containing trace data.
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                              the requests method.

Returns:
    PushTracesResponse: The response object.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.create_request_and_push_trace
documentation:
Push trace data.

Args:
    messages (List[List[Dict]]): The messages containing the trace data.
    annotations (Optional[List[List[Dict]]]): The annotations corresponding to the messages.
    metadata (Optional[List[Dict]]): The metadata corresponding to the messages.
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                              the requests method.

Returns:
    PushTracesResponse: The response object.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.get_dataset_metadata
documentation:
Get the metadata for a dataset.

Args:
    dataset_name (str): The name of the dataset to get metadata for.
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                              the requests method.

Returns:
    Dict: The response from the API.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.update_dataset_metadata
documentation:
Update the metadata for a dataset.

Args:
    request (UpdateDatasetMetadataRequest): The request object containing the dataset name,
                                            and metadata to update.
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                                        the requests method.

Returns:
    Dict: The response from the API.

methods.name: invariant_sdk.client.Client.create_request_and_update_dataset_metadata
documentation:
Update the metadata for a dataset.

Args:
    dataset_name (str): The name of the dataset to update metadata for.
    metadata (Dict): The metadata to update. The keys should be the metadata fields. 
                     Allowed fields are "benchmark", "accuracy", and "name".
    request_kwargs (Optional[Mapping]): Additional keyword arguments to pass to
                                        the requests method.

Returns:
    Dict: The response from the API.
Client for interacting with the Invariant APIs.
